behavior_summary: This script implements the item's/NPC's 'U8P_TREE::look' (examine) handler. When the player examines the TREE object it displays a short label/bark 'tree ' to the player and then yields (suspends) the process.
There is no branching, menu, flag read/write, or external calls; the behavior is a minimal descriptive response to the U8P_TREE::look action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TREE::look: Emit a single Bark/dialogue line 'tree ' and then execute a suspend/yield, ending or pausing the U8P_TREE::look process.
No conditional branches or subsequent actions are present in the process; control flow is linear: show text -> suspend.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'tree ' intentional or an artifact of extraction?
If the process is resumed after suspend, is there any engine-level follow-up not represented in this script? (No resume behavior is present in the decompiled fragment.)

main_function.name: U8P_TREE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle player 'U8P_TREE::look'/examine action for the TREE object: present a short descriptive bark and then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's U8P_TREE::look/examine event for the TREE object (process type 0x0208).
Invoked when the player inspects or targets the TREE using the 'U8P_TREE::look' action.

function_map.name: U8P_TREE::look
function_map.role: U8P_TREE::look handler that shows a short description/bark for the TREE object
